verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (TMP21/p24delta1) is a type I single-pass TM protein of the p24/EMP24/GP25L family. The NCBI summary explicitly states dual localization: 'localized to the plasma membrane and Golgi cisternae,' confirming a documented minority PM pool alongside a dominant Golgi/ERGIC residence involved in COPI/COPII vesicle trafficking. It cycles between ER, Golgi, and the plasma membrane as part of its trafficking function. The protein has its own TM domain and reaches the PM as part of this cycling. It is also a component of the gamma-secretase complex, which itself is a surface-resident multi-pass TM complex. The canonical compartment is endomembrane/Golgi, but the documented PM pool via secretory cycling qualifies as dual_localization contextual surface accessibility.
